2026-03-15T02:17:00.5212709Z     13.  * MELHORIAS DE CONDUCAO CIVICO:
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5213221Z     14.  *   1. CIVICO_D auto-degrade: quando dist < MEDIUM_DIST_M (25m), CIVICO_D usa
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5213732Z     15.  *      MISSION_34 (FollowCarFaraway) em vez de MISSION_43 (EscortRearFaraway).
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5214488Z     16.  *      MISSION_34 tem menos comportamento "chase" ao virar bruscamente perto do
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5215084Z     17.  *      recruta. Restaura MISSION_43 quando dist >= MEDIUM_DIST_M.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5215689Z     18.  *
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5216461Z     19.  *   2. Periodic road snap: JoinCarWithRoadSystem a cada ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL (5s)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5217320Z     20.  *      em modos CIVICO para manter alinhamento com os nos de estrada.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5217860Z     21.  *      Reduz desvios de faixa acumulados durante a conducao continuada.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5218206Z     22.  *
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5218735Z     23.  * NOTA: SLOW_ZONE re-snap REMOVIDO (causava INVALID_LINK → beelining).
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5219206Z     24.  * O snap periodico (ROAD_SNAP_INTERVAL) e suficiente para re-alinhar.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5219552Z     25.  */

2026-03-15T02:17:00.5220708Z     29. // DetectOffroad (throttled via g_offroadTimer)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5221141Z     30. // Devolve true se o veiculo esta mais de OFFROAD_DIST_M do no mais proximo.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5222052Z     31. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5222661Z     32. bool DetectOffroad(CVehicle* veh)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5222935Z     33. {
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5223121Z     34.     if (!veh) return true;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5223355Z     35. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5223545Z     36.     CNodeAddress node1, node2;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5223960Z     37.     CCarCtrl::FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo(veh, node1, node2);
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5224300Z     38. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5224505Z     39.     if (node1.IsEmpty()) return true;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5224759Z     40. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5225002Z     41.     CPathNode* pNode = ThePaths.GetPathNode(node1);
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5225385Z     42.     if (!pNode) return true;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5225811Z     43. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5226377Z     44.     CVector nodePos   = pNode->GetNodeCoors();
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5226960Z     45.     CVector vehicPos  = veh->GetPosition();
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5227235Z     46. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5227475Z     47.     return Dist2D(vehicPos, nodePos) > OFFROAD_DIST_M;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5227778Z     48. }
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5228059Z     49. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5228444Z     50. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5228840Z     51. // AdaptiveSpeed: ajusta velocidade ao angulo da curva.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5229303Z     52. // CCarCtrl::FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(straightLine) →
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5229702Z     53. //   mult 1.0 em reta, < 1.0 em curva.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5230108Z     54. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5230534Z     55. unsigned char AdaptiveSpeed(CVehicle* veh, unsigned char baseSpeed)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5230894Z     56. {
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5231085Z     57.     if (!veh) return baseSpeed;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5231366Z     58.     CAutoPilot& ap = veh->m_autoPilot;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5231869Z     59.     float mult = CCarCtrl::FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(ap.m_nStraightLineDistance);
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5232615Z     60.     mult = std::max(0.0f, std::min(1.0f, mult));
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5233437Z     61.     auto ideal = static_cast<unsigned char>(static_cast<float>(baseSpeed) * mult);
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5234238Z     62.     return std::max(ideal, SPEED_MIN);
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5234709Z     63. }
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5235007Z     64. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5235659Z     65. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5236653Z     66. // ApplyLaneAlignment: calcula targetHeading clipado ao road-graph.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5237530Z     67. // Devolve heading clipado ou heading actual se sem link valido.
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5238527Z     68. // (Valor devolvido usado apenas para logging — steering nao alterado.)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5239525Z     69. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5240173Z     70. float ApplyLaneAlignment(CVehicle* veh)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5240668Z     71. {
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5240995Z     72.     if (!veh) return 0.0f;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5241483Z     73.     CAutoPilot& ap = veh->m_autoPilot;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5241956Z     74. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5242366Z     75.     float currentHeading = veh->GetHeading();
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5242908Z     76. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5243380Z     77.     if (ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId == 0 &&
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5244094Z     78.         ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nAreaId == 0)
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5244680Z     79.         return currentHeading;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5245130Z     80. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5245496Z     81.     CVector fwd = veh->GetForward();
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5246063Z     82.     float targetHeading = currentHeading;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5246778Z     83. 
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5247216Z     84.     CCarCtrl::ClipTargetOrientationToLink(
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5248035Z     85.         veh,
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5248438Z     86.         ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo,
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5248924Z     87.         ap.m_nCurrentLane,
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5249372Z     88.         &targetHeading,
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5249995Z     89.         fwd.x,
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5250354Z     90.         fwd.y
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5250693Z     91.     );
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5251038Z     92.     return targetHeading;
2026-03-15T02:17:00.5251450Z     93. }

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9150662Z     101. //   1.o passe: prefere carros ja alinhados com o road-graph (linkId valido)

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9154262Z     105. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9155172Z     106. CVehicle* FindNearestFreeCar(CVector const& searchPos, CVehicle* excludePlayerCar)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9155947Z     107. {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9156533Z     108.     CVehicle* bestSnapped  = nullptr;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9157012Z     109.     float     bestSnappedD = FIND_CAR_RADIUS;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9157301Z     110. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9157545Z     111.     CVehicle* bestAny      = nullptr;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9157880Z     112.     float     bestAnyD     = FIND_CAR_RADIUS;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9158159Z     113. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9158442Z     114.     for (CVehicle* veh : *CPools::ms_pVehiclePool)

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9158985Z     116.         if (!veh)                        continue;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9159311Z     117.         if (veh == excludePlayerCar)     continue;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9159646Z     118.         if (veh->m_pDriver)              continue;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9160077Z     119.         if (veh->m_nVehicleSubClass > 2) continue;   // excluir avioes/helicopteros
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9160470Z     120. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9160724Z     121.         float d = Dist2D(veh->GetPosition(), searchPos);
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9161028Z     122. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9161215Z     123.         // Passe "qualquer"
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9161616Z     124.         if (d < bestAnyD)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9161984Z     125.         {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9162195Z     126.             bestAnyD = d;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9162431Z     127.             bestAny  = veh;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9162662Z     128.         }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9162962Z     129. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9163595Z     130.         // Passe "snapped": linkId valido → carro ja no road-graph
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9164432Z     131.         unsigned linkId = (unsigned)veh->m_autoPilot.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9164970Z     132.         if (linkId <= MAX_VALID_LINK_ID && d < bestSnappedD)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9165433Z     133.         {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9165644Z     134.             bestSnappedD = d;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9165907Z     135.             bestSnapped  = veh;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9166416Z     136.         }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9166733Z     137.     }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9167034Z     138. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9167509Z     139.     // Preferir carro snapped; fallback para qualquer carro disponivel
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9168282Z     140.     CVehicle* best     = bestSnapped ? bestSnapped : bestAny;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9168999Z     141.     float     bestDist = bestSnapped ? bestSnappedD : bestAnyD;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9169598Z     142. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9169922Z     143.     if (best)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9170283Z     144.     {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9170939Z     145.         unsigned linkId = (unsigned)best->m_autoPilot.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9171960Z     146.         LogEvent("FindNearestFreeCar: veh=%p dist=%.1fm linkId=%u(%s) pos=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f) %s",
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9172477Z     147.             static_cast<void*>(best), bestDist,
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9173008Z     148.             linkId, (linkId <= MAX_VALID_LINK_ID) ? "OK" : "INVALID",
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9173843Z     149.             best->GetPosition().x, best->GetPosition().y, best->GetPosition().z,
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9175067Z     150.             bestSnapped ? "(road-snapped preferido)" : "(sem carro snapped — qualquer aceite)");
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9176079Z     151.     }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9176578Z     152.     else
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9176902Z     153.     {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9177576Z     154.         LogWarn("FindNearestFreeCar: nenhum carro livre num raio de %.0fm", FIND_CAR_RADIUS);
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9178559Z     155.     }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9178868Z     156. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9179176Z     157.     return best;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9179539Z     158. }

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9231233Z     287. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9231735Z     288. void ProcessDrivingAI(CPlayerPed* player)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9232014Z     289. {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9232203Z     290.     if (!IsCarValid()) return;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9232444Z     291. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9232745Z     292.     CVehicle*   veh       = g_car;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9233046Z     293.     CAutoPilot& ap        = veh->m_autoPilot;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9233377Z     294.     CVector     vPos      = veh->GetPosition();
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9233728Z     295.     CVector     playerPos = player->GetPosition();
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9234083Z     296.     float       dist      = Dist2D(vPos, playerPos);
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9234375Z     297. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9234718Z     298.     // ── ZONA STOP: recruta completamente parado ──────────────────
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9235073Z     299.     if (dist < STOP_ZONE_M)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9235329Z     300.     {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9235442Z 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9235515Z function:
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9235691Z   name: view
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9235871Z   args:
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9236348Z     path: /home/runner/work/tentativa1/tentativa1/grove_recruit_asi/teste/source/Main.cpp
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9236786Z     view_range: |
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9236976Z       [
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9237142Z         80,
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9237322Z         250
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9237488Z       ]
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9237653Z   result: |
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9237869Z     80.     if (!g_recruit->IsAlive()) return false;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9238153Z     81.     return true;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9238363Z     82. }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9238528Z     83.

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9238919Z     85. {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9239109Z     86.     if (!g_car) return false;

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9239813Z     88.     return true;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9240014Z     89. }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9240177Z     90. 
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9240395Z     91. float Dist2D(CVector const& a, CVector const& b)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9240685Z     92. {
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9240873Z     93.     float dx = a.x - b.x;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9241115Z     94.     float dy = a.y - b.y;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9241382Z     95.     return std::sqrtf(dx * dx + dy * dy);
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9241656Z     96. }
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9241820Z     97.

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9245666Z     112. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9261078Z     126.                 player->GetPosition().x, player->GetPosition().y, player->GetPosition().z,

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9263709Z     134.             CVector spawnPos;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9264068Z     135.             spawnPos.x = pPos.x - std::sinf(heading) * SPAWN_BEHIND_DIST;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9264532Z     136.             spawnPos.y = pPos.y - std::cosf(heading) * SPAWN_BEHIND_DIST;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9264891Z     137.             spawnPos.z = pPos.z;
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9265134Z     138.

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9265833Z     140.                 static_cast<unsigned int>(modelIdx),

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9266520Z     142.             if (!ped)
2026-03-15T02:17:04.9266735Z     143.             {

2026-03-15T02:17:04.9268795Z     149.             LogEvent("KEY 1 (RECRUIT): ped criado %p modelo=%d pedType=%d (GSF=8) spawnPos=(%.1f,%.1f,%.1f)",

2026-03-15T02:19:02.8685468Z Now I'm checking what these mission types actually do. MISSION_43 appears to be an escort/follow behavior for distant targets, while MISSION_34 is a closer-range follow that's more aggressive about path-finding. The user's observation that the NPC follows the correct path when far away but goes wrong when close confirms this is the distance-based switching causing the problem.
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8687151Z 
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8688768Z The solution is to keep the distant-follow behavior (MISSION_43) active throughout the CIVICO_D state and just throttle the speed when the recruit gets too close, rather than switching to a different mission type that ignores road constraints. to minimize the "chase" behavior without switching missions

2026-03-15T02:19:02.8691306Z

This is the "last mile" behavior. The car AI, when very close to its target, might decide to go straight for it instead of following the road.
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8694125Z

2026-03-15T02:19:02.8695376Z 3. The SLOW_ZONE (10m) and STOP_ZONE (6m) already handle the very close case

2026-03-15T02:19:02.8884154Z     352. 
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8884390Z     353.     CPlayerPed* player = CWorld::Players[0].m_pPed;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8884710Z     354.     if (!player) return;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8884934Z     355. 
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8885113Z     356.     HandleKeys(player);
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8885335Z     357. 
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8885618Z     358.     // Observador vanilla: loga estado do motor do jogo (throttled ~2s)
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8886090Z     359.     // Activo sempre (mesmo INACTIVE) para capturar comportamento do NPC
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8886765Z     360.     // de trafego e peds GSF sem o mod interferir — referencia de diagnostico.
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8887160Z     361.     ProcessObserver(player);
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8887400Z     362. 
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8887576Z     363.     switch (g_state)
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8887792Z     364.     {
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8887995Z     365.     case ModState::ON_FOOT:
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8888253Z     366.         ProcessOnFoot(player);
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8888505Z     367.         break;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8888727Z     368.     case ModState::ENTER_CAR:
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8888992Z     369.         ProcessEnterCar(player);
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8889262Z     370.         break;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8889472Z     371.     case ModState::DRIVING:
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8889726Z     372.         ProcessDriving(player);
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8889972Z     373.         break;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8890183Z     374.     case ModState::PASSENGER:
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8890444Z     375.         ProcessPassenger(player);
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8890696Z     376.         break;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8890906Z     377.     case ModState::INACTIVE:
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8891326Z     378.     default:
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8891537Z     379.         break;
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8891728Z     380.     }
2026-03-15T02:19:02.8891915Z     381. }

2026-03-15T02:21:17.4176583Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4177112Z 1. **The auto-degrade from MISSION_43 to MISSION_34 is being triggered** (at dist=24.9m, frame 21441 and 23306). The recruit keeps flipping between MISSION_43 and MISSION_34.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4177725Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4178389Z 2. **physSpeed=0kmh in many places** - The recruit is often completely stopped (physSpeed=0) even though speed_ap=38. This means the car is stopped despite having a mission to follow. This could be because:
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4179409Z    - WRONG_DIR condition: The car is heading the wrong way and stops to reorient
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4179918Z    - The road graph mission might be causing the car to stop and wait
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4180199Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4180895Z 3. **WRONG_DIR is the core problem** - The recruit car heading is nearly opposite to the targetHeading almost all the time. `heading=-1.974 targetH=2.323 deltaH=-1.986(WRONG_DIR!)` - this is happening repeatedly.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4181674Z

2026-03-15T02:21:17.4182594Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4183074Z 5. **Looking at the speed multiplier and straight-line distance metrics** to understand how the speed calculations are affecting the recruit's behavior.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4183663Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4185083Z 6. **The car stops when facing the wrong direction** - this is GTA SA's normal behavior when a vehicle is going the wrong way on a road-graph path, so it stops and attempts to reverse or re-route. The core issue is that the recruit is consistently heading in the wrong direction, which triggers this stopping behavior.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4186438Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4188616Z Looking at the performance data, vanilla traffic NPCs maintain speed_ap between 13-19 with physSpeed of 27-38km/h and occasionally encounter WRONG_DIR, but the recruit NPC shows speed_ap of 38 with physSpeed fluctuating wildly between 0-54km/h and WRONG_DIR occurring much more frequently. At frame 22858, the recruit reaches 54km/h despite a cruise speed of 38, then drops to 0 shortly after. The pattern I'm noticing is a rapid mission switch at frames 21418-21442 where the car degrades from chase mode to road-follow, then immediately restores back to chase mode - this oscillation might be causing the erratic behavior.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4190781Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4193417Z The recruit is stuck oscillating right at the 25m threshold, constantly flipping between the two missions every frame. This constant mission switching is forcing the AutoPilot to re-route repeatedly, which explains why the car keeps stopping and veering off-road. The root issue is this auto-degrade oscillation disrupting navigation, and the fix would be to either remove the auto-degrade entirely and keep the chase mission active, or adjust the threshold distance to prevent this boundary condition. SPEED_CIVICO** from 38 to match vanilla traffic (13-19 range), maybe 25 as a compromise.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4197323Z 3. **Remove respect boost** - it's not helping and breaks vanilla behavior
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4197931Z 4. **Add hysteresis to auto-degrade** if I keep it, though removing it entirely might be cleaner

2026-03-15T02:21:17.4198802Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4201800Z From the logs, I'm seeing that after the oscillation settles, the mission stabilizes at state 53, then the car re-orients at frame 23432. The recruit is stopping frequently even when it should be moving—this happens when WRONG_DIR is active and the road-graph AI forces a stop. The issue seems to be in `ApplyLaneAlignment`, but `targetH=2.323` stays almost constant, which means `ClipTargetOrientationToLink` is locking to the same heading every frame. The car's actual heading varies, but when it drifts the wrong way (heading around -1.974 versus target 2.323), the delta exceeds the WRONG_DIR_THRESHOLD and the game stops it.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4204228Z 
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4205780Z Interestingly, vanilla traffic also shows WRONG_DIR sometimes—that's just normal behavior. The real problem is that the recruit car can't turn fast enough at speed 38 to correct course, so it gets stuck going the wrong way and the road-graph keeps it stopped. Lowering the speed to around 22-25 should let it execute turns properly and avoid this trap.
2026-03-15T02:21:17.4207350Z

The real fix is to remove that auto-degrade block entirely—it's what's causing the oscillation between mission states. I'll also lower the base speed from 38 to 22 to better match vanilla traffic patterns, and make sure the physics speed gets logged so I can verify the behavior is actually improving.

2026-03-15T02:26:08.0961753Z     106. CVehicle* FindNearestFreeCar(CVector const& searchPos, CVehicle* excludePlayerCar)
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0962513Z     107. {

2026-03-15T02:26:08.0964037Z     110. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0964371Z     111.     CVehicle* bestAny      = nullptr;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0964891Z     112.     float     bestAnyD     = FIND_CAR_RADIUS;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0965358Z     113. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0965762Z     114.     for (CVehicle* veh : *CPools::ms_pVehiclePool)
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0966506Z     115.     {
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0966879Z     116.         if (!veh)                        continue;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0967429Z     117.         if (veh == excludePlayerCar)     continue;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0967982Z     118.         if (veh->m_pDriver)              continue;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0968715Z     119.         if (veh->m_nVehicleSubClass > 2) continue;   // excluir avioes/helicopteros
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0969398Z     120. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0969810Z     121.         float d = Dist2D(veh->GetPosition(), searchPos);
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0970361Z     122. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0970679Z     123.         // Passe "qualquer"
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0971108Z     124.         if (d < bestAnyD)
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0971510Z     125.         {
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0971841Z     126.             bestAnyD = d;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0972225Z     127.             bestAny  = veh;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0972619Z     128.         }
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0972919Z     129. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0973515Z     130.         // Passe "snapped": linkId valido → carro ja no road-graph
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0974434Z     131.         unsigned linkId = (unsigned)veh->m_autoPilot.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0975390Z     132.         if (linkId <= MAX_VALID_LINK_ID && d < bestSnappedD)
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0975912Z     133.         {
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0976553Z     134.             bestSnappedD = d;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0977008Z     135.             bestSnapped  = veh;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0977430Z     136.         }
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0977746Z     137.     }
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0978053Z     138. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0978550Z     139.     // Preferir carro snapped; fallback para qualquer carro disponivel
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0979336Z     140.     CVehicle* best     = bestSnapped ? bestSnapped : bestAny;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0980080Z     141.     float     bestDist = bestSnapped ? bestSnappedD : bestAnyD;
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0980640Z     142. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0980937Z     143.     if (best)
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0981262Z     144.     {

2026-03-15T02:26:08.0983878Z     147.             static_cast<void*>(best), bestDist,
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0984520Z     148.             linkId, (linkId <= MAX_VALID_LINK_ID) ? "OK" : "INVALID",
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0985346Z     149.             best->GetPosition().x, best->GetPosition().y, best->GetPosition().z,
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0986691Z     150.             bestSnapped ? "(road-snapped preferido)" : "(sem carro snapped — qualquer aceite)");
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0987467Z     151.     }
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0987783Z     152.     else
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0988101Z     153.     {
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0988744Z     154.         LogWarn("FindNearestFreeCar: nenhum carro livre num raio de %.0fm", FIND_CAR_RADIUS);
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0989509Z     155.     }
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0989803Z     156. 
2026-03-15T02:26:08.0990111Z     157.     return best;

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4689769Z         float current = CStats::GetStatValue(STAT_RESPECT);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4690316Z         if (current < RESPECT_TEST_BOOST)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4690573Z         {
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4690760Z             g_savedRespect = current;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4691083Z             CStats::SetStatValue(STAT_RESPECT, RESPECT_TEST_BOOST);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4691469Z             LogEvent("RESPECT_BOOST: ACTIVADO %.0f -> %.0f "
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4692252Z                      "(persistente: cobre MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup + CreateFirstSubTask deferred + rescan frames)",
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4693111Z                 current, RESPECT_TEST_BOOST);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4693543Z         }
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4693824Z         else

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4694430Z             g_savedRespect = -1.0f;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4695180Z             LogEvent("RESPECT_BOOST: desnecessario (respect=%.0f >= %.0f)", current, RESPECT_TEST_BOOST);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4695952Z         }
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4696725Z     }

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4710893Z     // AddRecruitToGroup
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4711277Z     // Sequencia completa de entrada no grupo + follow.
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4711644Z     // Equivalente ao bloco CLEO (0631 + 087F + 0961 + 06F0 + 0850).
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4712093Z     // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4712431Z     void AddRecruitToGroup(CPlayerPed* player)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4712695Z     {
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4712879Z         if (!player || !g_recruit) return;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4713118Z     
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4713435Z         // ── Passo 1: Flags ANTES de entrar no grupo ──────────────────
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4713816Z         g_recruit->bNeverLeavesGroup                  = 1;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4714159Z         g_recruit->bKeepTasksAfterCleanUp             = 1;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4714507Z         g_recruit->bDoesntListenToPlayerGroupCommands = 0;

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4715900Z         if (groupIdx < 8u)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4716109Z         {
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4716492Z             int slotBefore = FindRecruitMemberID(player);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4716800Z             if (slotBefore < 0)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4717023Z             {

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4717857Z                     int existGi = -1, existSi = -1;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4718172Z                     for (int gi = 0; gi < 8 && existGi < 0; ++gi)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4718480Z                         for (int si = 0; si < 7; ++si)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4718903Z                             if (CPedGroups::ms_groups[gi].m_groupMembership.m_apMembers[si] == g_recruit)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4719360Z                             { existGi = gi; existSi = si; break; }
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4719642Z     
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4719859Z                     int maxMem = FindMaxGroupMembers();
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4720204Z                     float resp  = CStats::GetStatValue(STAT_RESPECT);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4720713Z                     LogGroup("PRE_JOIN: ped_em_grupo=%d(slot=%d) FindMaxGroupMembers=%d respect=%.0f playerGrp=%u "
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4721166Z                              "(%s)",
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4721455Z                         existGi, existSi, maxMem, resp, groupIdx,
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4722011Z                         existGi >= 0 ? "ATENCAO: ped JA tem grupo — removendo antes de MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup" :
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4722530Z                                        "ped sem grupo (OK para MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup)");
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4722866Z     
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4723205Z                     // Remove do grupo existente para que MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup possa aceitar o ped.
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4723773Z                     // GSF NPCs (pedType=8) sao automaticamente colocados em grupos de gang internos;
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4724318Z                     // sem esta remocao previa, MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup falha silenciosamente.
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4724719Z                     if (existGi >= 0)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4724959Z                     {

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4726506Z                             existGi, existSi);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4726755Z                     }
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4726943Z                 }
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4727122Z     
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4727344Z                 player->MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup(g_recruit);

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4728141Z                 if (slotAfter < 0)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4728366Z                 {
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4728690Z                     // Backup direto via AddFollower
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4729106Z                     CPedGroups::ms_groups[groupIdx].m_groupMembership.AddFollower(g_recruit);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4729553Z                     slotAfter = FindRecruitMemberID(player);
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4729846Z                     if (slotAfter < 0)
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4730083Z                     {
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4730641Z                         LogWarn("AddRecruitToGroup: MakeThisPedJoinOurGroup E AddFollower falharam — recruta fora do grupo!");
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4731147Z                     }
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4731338Z                     else
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4731541Z                     {

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4732329Z                         // CPedGroupIntelligence. ComputeDefaultTasks nao o encontra e nao
2026-03-15T02:27:58.4732810Z                         // atribui GANG_FOLLOWER. g_joinedViaAddFollower=true marca para

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4735440Z                             (int)g_recruit->m_nPedType, slotAfter);

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4736746Z                         // o que deixa m_nDecisionMakerTypeInGroup=UNKNOWN e faz CreateFirstSubTask

2026-03-15T02:27:58.4739459Z                         void* pIntel2 = GetGroupIntelligence(gIdx2);

2026-03-15T02:30:16.7258715Z - For the respect bitmask, I'm looking at the `CPedAcquaintance` structure from gta-reversed—it stores respect as a bitmask where each bit represents whether the ped respects a particular ped type, with bit 0 corresponding to the player. I need to set that bit directly on the recruit's acquaintance data rather than going through the stat system. So the fix is to set `g_recruit->m_acquaintance.m_nRespect |= 1u` directly in `AddRecruitToGroup`. Now I'm looking at what `m_acquaintance` actually is — it's a `CAcquaintance` struct member on `CPed` that stores bitmasks for different relationship types, with `nRespect` tracking which ped groups this ped respects, where each bit corresponds to a ped group index and bit 0 represents the player's ped type.
2026-03-15T02:30:16.7262025Z 
2026-03-15T02:30:16.7263151Z I'm checking how the respect check actually works — it's likely either shifting the bitmask by the ped type and checking the bit, or using a bitwise AND with a shifted value to determine if one ped respects another.
2026-03-15T02:30:16.7264248Z

2026-03-15T02:30:38.9603604Z     // is separate pedtype, starting from 0 (PLAYER1) to 31 (MISSION8)) behaves toward any ped of other type.
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9604675Z     // Five dwords in this structure (only four of them are used) are the bitmasks,
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9605595Z     // where a bit sets a flag of acquaintance type (respect, like, dislike, hate)
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9606562Z     // to the pedtype of given index (=number of bit).
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9607327Z     // Say, if the 4th bit (counting from zero) in PedRelationship[5].respect is set,
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9608347Z     // it means that a ped of type CIVFEMALE (pedtype=5) respects a ped of type CIVMALE (pedtype=4).
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9609119Z     //
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9609603Z     // These structures are filled with data from the file ped.dat.
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9610712Z     // The relationship flags could be changed using opcode 0746 and similar.
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9611373Z     //
2026-03-15T02:30:38.9612110Z     // https://gtamods.com/wiki/Saves_(GTA_SA)#Block_19:_Ped_Relationships

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9155359Z ```cpp

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9155747Z     Plugin-SDK file

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9157576Z #include "Base.h"
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9157692Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9157791Z typedef int32 AcquaintanceId;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9158015Z enum {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9158198Z     ACQUAINTANCE_RESPECT = 0,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9158430Z     ACQUAINTANCE_LIKE    = 1,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9158655Z     ACQUAINTANCE_IGNORE  = 2,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9158882Z     ACQUAINTANCE_DISLIKE = 3,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9159108Z     ACQUAINTANCE_HATE    = 4,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9159254Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9159336Z     ACQUAINTANCE_NUM
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9159528Z };
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9159618Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9159916Z // This structure stores information about how a ped of given pedtype (each entry in PedRelationship[32]
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9160597Z // is separate pedtype, starting from 0 (PLAYER1) to 31 (MISSION8)) behaves toward any ped of other type.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9161212Z // Five dwords in this structure (only four of them are used) are the bitmasks,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9161825Z // where a bit sets a flag of acquaintance type (respect, like, dislike, hate)
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9162233Z // to the pedtype of given index (=number of bit).
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9162636Z // Say, if the 4th bit (counting from zero) in PedRelationship[5].respect is set,
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9163182Z // it means that a ped of type CIVFEMALE (pedtype=5) respects a ped of type CIVMALE (pedtype=4).
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9163595Z //
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9163869Z // These structures are filled with data from the file ped.dat.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9164303Z // The relationship flags could be changed using opcode 0746 and similar.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9164658Z //
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9165206Z // https://gtamods.com/wiki/Saves_(GTA_SA)#Block_19:_Ped_Relationships
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9165572Z class CAcquaintance {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9165782Z public:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9165953Z     union {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9166293Z         struct {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9166694Z             uint32 m_nRespect;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9166929Z             uint32 m_nLike;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9167149Z             uint32 m_nIgnore;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9167369Z             uint32 m_nDislike;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9167591Z             uint32 m_nHate;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9167798Z         };
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9168023Z         std::array<uint32, 5> m_acquaintances;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9168282Z     };

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9193498Z ### 3. **Respects() Function** (From PedIntelligence.cpp)
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9193792Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9194018Z bool CPedIntelligence::Respects(CPed* ped) const {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9194369Z     auto respect = m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect();
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9194738Z     auto pedFlag = CPedType::GetPedFlag(ped->m_nPedType);
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9195048Z     return pedFlag & respect;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9195264Z }
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9195450Z ```

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9195837Z ## Detailed Analysis Answers
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9196034Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9196396Z ### Q1: What is the m_acquaintance struct in CPed?
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9196674Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9197089Z The `m_acquaintance` member in `CPed` is a **`CAcquaintance` object** that stores ped relationship flags. It's declared in `CPed` as:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9197622Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9197811Z CAcquaintance m_acquaintance;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9198033Z ```
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9198124Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9198233Z **CAcquaintance Structure** (20 bytes / 0x14):
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9198697Z - A union of 5 uint32 members (4 are actively used):
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9199060Z   - `uint32 m_nRespect` - bitmask for peds this ped respects
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9199552Z   - `uint32 m_nLike` - bitmask for peds this ped likes
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9199899Z   - `uint32 m_nIgnore` - bitmask for peds this ped ignores
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9200368Z   - `uint32 m_nDislike` - bitmask for peds this ped dislikes
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9200714Z   - `uint32 m_nHate` - bitmask for peds this ped hates
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9201169Z   - Accessible as `std::array<uint32, 5> m_acquaintances`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9201402Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9201804Z Each bit position (0-31) in these uint32s represents a ped type (0 = PLAYER1, 1 = PLAYER2, etc., up to 31 = MISSION8).
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9202254Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9202403Z ### Q2: How does Respects() work (what bitmask does it check)?
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9202756Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9202852Z The `Respects()` function:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9203273Z 1. Gets the **respect bitmask** from the calling ped: `respect = m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect()`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9204096Z 2. Gets the **ped flag** for the target ped using its ped type: `pedFlag = CPedType::GetPedFlag(ped->m_nPedType)`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9204838Z 3. Returns true if the flag bit is set in the respect bitmask: `return pedFlag & respect`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9205275Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9205360Z **Bitmask mechanism:**
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9205672Z - `GetPedFlag(ePedType)` creates a flag: `return 1 << (size_t)pedType`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9206684Z - The & operation checks if that bit is set in the respect mask
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9207496Z - Example: If target ped has `m_nPedType = PED_TYPE_CIVMALE (4)`, the flag is `1 << 4 = 0x10`. If the respect mask has bit 4 set, `Respects()` returns true.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9208255Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9208408Z ### Q3: What bit index corresponds to PEDTYPE_PLAYER1?
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9208626Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9208749Z **Bit index 0 (zero)**
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9208955Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9209045Z From `ePedType` enum:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9209242Z ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9209422Z enum ePedType : uint32 {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9209756Z     PED_TYPE_NONE = (uint32)(-1),
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9210259Z     PED_TYPE_PLAYER1 = 0,    // ← Bit index 0
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9210711Z     PED_TYPE_PLAYER2,        // ← Bit index 1
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9210982Z     // ... more types ...
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9211200Z }
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9211455Z ```
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9211550Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9211872Z The `GetPedFlag()` function returns: `1 << PED_TYPE_PLAYER1 = 1 << 0 = 0x00000001` (only the least significant bit set).
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9212484Z git rev-parse HEAD

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9290638Z     // This structure stores information about how a ped of given pedtype (each entry in PedRelationship[32]
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9292034Z     // is separate pedtype, starting from 0 (PLAYER1) to 31 (MISSION8)) behaves toward any ped of other type.

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9293949Z     // where a bit sets a flag of acquaintance type (respect, like, dislike, hate)
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9294661Z     // to the pedtype of given index (=number of bit).

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9296602Z     // it means that a ped of type CIVFEMALE (pedtype=5) respects a ped of type CIVMALE (pedtype=4).
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9297372Z     //
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9297830Z     // These structures are filled with data from the file ped.dat.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9298649Z     // The relationship flags could be changed using opcode 0746 and similar.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9299279Z     //
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9299774Z     // https://gtamods.com/wiki/Saves_(GTA_SA)#Block_19:_Ped_Relationships

2026-03-15T02:31:22.9344807Z     bool CPedIntelligence::Respects(CPed* ped) const {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9345454Z         auto respect = m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect();
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9346503Z         auto pedFlag = CPedType::GetPedFlag(ped->m_nPedType);
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9347129Z         return pedFlag & respect;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9347539Z     }
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9347819Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9348094Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9348385Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9348668Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9348977Z     ## Detailed Analysis Answers
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9349379Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9349745Z     ### Q1: What is the m_acquaintance struct in CPed?
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9350236Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9351090Z     The `m_acquaintance` member in `CPed` is a **`CAcquaintance` object** that stores ped relationship flags. It's declared in `CPed` as:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9352069Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9352399Z     CAcquaintance m_acquaintance;
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9352814Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9353100Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9353684Z     **CAcquaintance Structure** (20 bytes / 0x14):
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9354280Z     - A union of 5 uint32 members (4 are actively used):
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9354947Z       - `uint32 m_nRespect` - bitmask for peds this ped respects
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9355772Z       - `uint32 m_nLike` - bitmask for peds this ped likes
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9356894Z       - `uint32 m_nIgnore` - bitmask for peds this ped ignores
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9357570Z       - `uint32 m_nDislike` - bitmask for peds this ped dislikes
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9358224Z       - `uint32 m_nHate` - bitmask for peds this ped hates
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9358864Z       - Accessible as `std::array<uint32, 5> m_acquaintances`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9359396Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9360155Z     Each bit position (0-31) in these uint32s represents a ped type (0 = PLAYER1, 1 = PLAYER2, etc., up to 31 = MISSION8).
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9361037Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9361456Z     ### Q2: How does Respects() work (what bitmask does it check)?
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9362009Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9362318Z     The `Respects()` function:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9363090Z     1. Gets the **respect bitmask** from the calling ped: `respect = m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect()`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9364341Z     2. Gets the **ped flag** for the target ped using its ped type: `pedFlag = CPedType::GetPedFlag(ped->m_nPedType)`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9365477Z     3. Returns true if the flag bit is set in the respect bitmask: `return pedFlag & respect`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9366363Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9366674Z     **Bitmask mechanism:**
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9367266Z     - `GetPedFlag(ePedType)` creates a flag: `return 1 << (size_t)pedType`
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9368071Z     - The & operation checks if that bit is set in the respect mask
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9369295Z     - Example: If target ped has `m_nPedType = PED_TYPE_CIVMALE (4)`, the flag is `1 << 4 = 0x10`. If the respect mask has bit 4 set, `Respects()` returns true.
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9370345Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9370754Z     ### Q3: What bit index corresponds to PEDTYPE_PLAYER1?
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9371263Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9371565Z     **Bit index 0 (zero)**
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9371931Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9372243Z     From `ePedType` enum:
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9372629Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9372958Z     enum ePedType : uint32 {
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9373388Z         PED_TYPE_NONE = (uint32)(-1),
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9374121Z         PED_TYPE_PLAYER1 = 0,    // ← Bit index 0
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9374755Z         PED_TYPE_PLAYER2,        // ← Bit index 1
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9375243Z         // ... more types ...
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9375629Z     }
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9375920Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9376394Z     
2026-03-15T02:31:22.9377140Z     The `GetPedFlag()` function returns: `1 << PED_TYPE_PLAYER1 = 1 << 0 = 0x00000001` (only the least significant bit set).
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4893625Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4894392Z copilot: Excellent! Now I understand the full picture:
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4894771Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4895149Z 1. **Respects() fix**: The function `CPedIntelligence::Respects(CPed* ped)` checks:
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4896007Z    - Gets the **respect bitmask** from the recruit ped: `respect = m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect()`
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4897255Z    - Gets the **ped flag** for the target ped: `pedFlag = CPedType::GetPedFlag(ped->m_nPedType)` = `1 << ped->m_nPedType`
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4897894Z    - Returns `pedFlag & respect`
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4898168Z    
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4899363Z For the player (PEDTYPE_PLAYER1 = 0), I need bit 0 set in the respect bitmask, so I'd set it with a bitwise OR operation. However, there's a complication: the `SetAsAcquaintance` method clears all other acquaintance types when setting one, so I can't just modify the respect field directly without potentially wiping out the hate and dislike flags.
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4900553Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4903088Z The cleanest approach is to set the respect bit directly while clearing it from the hate and dislike masks if needed, since I don't have direct access to call the wrapper function anyway. Now I need to remove the ActivateRespect and DeactivateRespectBoost functions from grove_recruit_group.cpp, along with their declarations and the g_savedRespect globals from the header and Main.cpp. I also need to disable the CIVICO_D auto-degrade logic in ProcessDrivingAI that switches between missions, and update the drive style from DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS to DRIVINGSTYLE_FOLLOW_TRAFFIC_LAWS in SetupDriveMode. Let me check if CIVICO_E needs similar adjustments.
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4905671Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4908697Z Looking at the drive styles more carefully—DRIVINGSTYLE_FOLLOW_TRAFFIC_LAWS makes NPCs respect traffic lights and follow the road graph like vanilla behavior, while DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS just stops for other vehicles without strict road adherence, and DRIVINGSTYLE_AVOID_CARS causes the swerving that leads to sidewalk issues. So changing CIVICO to use DRIVINGSTYLE_FOLLOW_TRAFFIC_LAWS should apply across all its mission variants.
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4910403Z 
2026-03-15T02:31:52.4912306Z The real culprit for the beeline behavior near the player is actually the MISSION_34 auto-degrade, not the drive style itself—that's the chase mission that ignores the road graph entirely. So I need to remove that auto-degrade and update the drive style to match vanilla. 2026-03-15T02:34:00.0095276Z     155. 26479: 2026-03-15T02:04:15.1805019Z
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0095770Z     156. 26480: // This structure stores information about how a ped of given pedtype (each entry in PedRelationship[32]
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0096646Z     157. 26481: // is separate pedtype, starting from 0 (PLAYER1) to 31 (MISSION8)) behaves toward any ped of other type.
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0097285Z     158. 26482: // Five dwords in this structure (only four of them are used) are the bitmasks,
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0097842Z     159. 26483: // where a bit sets a flag of acquaintance type (respect, like, dislike, hate)
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0098307Z     160. 26484: // to the pedtype of given index (=number of bit).
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0098768Z     161. 26485: // Say, if the 4th bit (counting from zero) in PedRelationship[5].respect is set,
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0099379Z     162. 26486: // it means that a ped of type CIVFEMALE (pedtype=5) respects a ped of type CIVMALE (pedtype=4).
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0099833Z     163. 26487: //
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0100131Z     164. 26488: // These structures are filled with data from the file ped.dat.
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0100629Z     165. 26489: // The relationship flags could be changed using opcode 0746 and similar.
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0101011Z     166. 26490: //
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0101335Z     167. 26491: // https://gtamods.com/wiki/Saves_(GTA_SA)#Block_19:_Ped_Relationships
2026-03-15T02:34:00.0101731Z     168. 26492: class CAcquaintance {

2026-03-15T02:34:00.0102180Z     170. 26494: union {

The road-ignoring behavior at close range might stem from MISSION_34 causing direct steering toward the target instead of following the road graph, or from the STOP_ZONE triggering MISSION_STOP_FOREVER and then recovery logic picking the wrong behavior. The issue might be that when the recruit transitions between different mission states—especially exiting the slow and stop zones—the road-following behavior doesn't recover properly. But I'm noticing something in the vanilla NPC logs: it's using `driveStyle=4`, and I need to figure out what that driving style actually does in GTA SA's autopilot system, since the recruit seems to be using a different one. So the vanilla traffic uses `driveStyle=4` (FOLLOW_TRAFFIC_LAWS) based on the logs, which means our recruit should match that for proper road-following. If CIVICO is currently using `driveStyle=0` (STOP_FOR_CARS), it would just halt at obstacles, but the reported behavior of going on sidewalks and wrong lanes suggests it might actually be set to `driveStyle=1` (AVOID_CARS) instead, which would cause swerving around obstacles rather than following the road graph.

2026-03-15T02:35:20.9572567Z 
2026-03-15T02:35:20.9572572Z 
2026-03-15T02:35:20.9573018Z - The actual check is a bitwise AND: `m_nRespect & (1 << PED_TYPE_PLAYER1)` to see if bit 0 is set
2026-03-15T02:35:20.9574519Z - Fix: Set that bit directly when spawning the recruit with `g_recruit->m_acquaintance.m_nRespect |= 1u`, and clear it on dismiss; the respect boost functions are completely unnecessary

2026-03-15T02:35:20.9575891Z ### Problem 2: 
2026-03-15T02:35:20.9576103Z 
2026-03-15T02:35:20.9587050Z The CIVICO_D mission auto-downgrades to MISSION_34 when the player gets within 25 meters, and that chase mission ignores the road graph entirely—that's what causes the sidewalk-climbing behavior. I need to prevent that downgrade and keep MISSION_43 active, plus switch the driving style from stop-for-cars to follow-traffic-laws (value 4 based on what I'm seeing in the vanilla logs). But I'm running into conflicting info in the SDK docs about what the actual driving style values are. I'm looking at the enum definitions from the plugin-sdk to verify the drive style values, and there's a mismatch—vanilla traffic is set to `driveStyle=4`, which maps to `DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS_IGNORE_LIGHTS` according to the documentation, but that doesn't align with what was previously stated about vanilla traffic following traffic laws. I'm seeing conflicting enum definitions for the driving style values—the gta-reversed source lists `DRIVINGSTYLE_FOLLOW_TRAFFIC_LAWS = 4`, but the plugin-sdk analysis suggests `4 = STOP_FOR_CARS_IGNORE_LIGHTS`. The previous analysis concluded that vanilla traffic uses `driveStyle=4` based on actual game behavior, so I need to verify which enum is correct by checking the plugin-sdk directly. In the meantime, I'm tracking the three fixes that were identified: the respect boost logic in AddRecruitToGroup, removing the CIVICO_D auto-degrade transition, and adjusting the CIVICO driving style value. I'm cross-referencing the plugin-sdk enum values with the gta-reversed analysis, and it looks like value 4 is `DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS_IGNORE_LIGHTS` — they stop for obstacles but ignore traffic lights. For vanilla NPC traffic, this makes sense since they generally stop for cars and pedestrians but drive through red lights.

2026-03-15T02:35:26.0193836Z     161. // SetupDriveMode
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0194191Z     162. // Configura o CAutoPilot do carro do recruta para o modo escolhido.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0194690Z     163. // Chamado uma vez ao mudar de modo (KEY 4) ou ao transitar para DRIVING.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0195276Z     164. //
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0195624Z     165. // IMPORTANTE: JoinCarWithRoadSystem e o passo critico que snap ao
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0196105Z     166. // road-graph. Sem este passo o NPC pode navegar fora da estrada.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0196863Z     167. // Equivalente ao opcode CLEO 06E1 que chama esta funcao internamente.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0197441Z     168. // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0197837Z     169. void SetupDriveMode(CPlayerPed* player, DriveMode mode)
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0198155Z     170. {

2026-03-15T02:35:26.0198683Z     172. 
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0198882Z     173.     CVehicle*   recruitCar = g_car;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0199209Z     174.     CAutoPilot& ap         = recruitCar->m_autoPilot;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0199649Z     175.     CVehicle*   playerCar  = player->bInVehicle ? player->m_pVehicle : nullptr;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0200036Z     176. 
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0200216Z     177.     switch (mode)
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0200479Z     178.     {
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0200837Z     179.     // ── CIVICO-D: EscortRearFaraway ─────────────────────────────
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0201253Z     180.     // Usa road-graph identico ao NPC de escolta vanilla.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0201632Z     181.     // m_pTargetCar aponta para o carro do jogador.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0202060Z     182.     // NOTA: ProcessDrivingAI pode baixar para MISSION_34 a curta distancia.
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0202477Z     183.     case DriveMode::CIVICO_D:
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0202724Z     184.     {
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0202916Z     185.         if (!playerCar)
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0203145Z     186.         {
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0203495Z     187.             LogDrive("SetupDriveMode: CIVICO_D sem carro jogador -> fallback DIRETO");
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0203967Z     188.             SetupDriveMode(player, DriveMode::DIRETO);
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0204267Z     189.             return;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0204474Z     190.         }
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0204761Z     191.         ap.m_nCarMission      = MISSION_43;  // EscortRearFaraway (=67)
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0205147Z     192.         ap.m_pTargetCar       = playerCar;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0205459Z     193.         ap.m_nCruiseSpeed     = SPEED_CIVICO;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0205812Z     194.         ap.m_nCarDrivingStyle = DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0206347Z     195.         {
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0206896Z     196.             unsigned linkPre  = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0207440Z     197.             unsigned areaPre  = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nAreaId;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0207878Z     198.             float    headPre  = recruitCar->GetHeading();
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0208249Z     199.             float    playerH  = playerCar->GetHeading();
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0208700Z     200.             CCarCtrl::JoinCarWithRoadSystem(recruitCar);
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0209253Z     201.             g_civicRoadSnapTimer = 0;   // reset snap timer apos JoinRoad no SetupDriveMode
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0209984Z     202.             unsigned linkPost = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nCarPathLinkId;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0210503Z     203.             unsigned areaPost = (unsigned)ap.m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo.m_nAreaId;

2026-03-15T02:35:26.0212218Z     206.                      "JOIN_ROAD: linkId %u->%u areaId %u->%u heading_pre=%.3f heading_post=%.3f lane=%d "
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0212679Z     207.                      "playerHeading=%.3f (%s)",
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0213041Z     208.                 (int)ap.m_nCruiseSpeed, static_cast<void*>(playerCar),
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0213425Z     209.                 linkPre, linkPost, areaPre, areaPost,
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0213775Z     210.                 headPre, headPost, (int)ap.m_nCurrentLane,
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0214088Z     211.                 playerH,
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0214510Z     212.                 (linkPre == linkPost ? "ATENCAO: linkId nao mudou apos JoinRoad!" : "JoinRoad OK snap"));
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0214946Z     213.         }
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0215141Z     214.         break;
2026-03-15T02:35:26.0215340Z     215.     }

2026-03-15T02:36:30.4023913Z     # grove_recruit_asi — Análise CLEO vs plugin-sdk e Protótipo ASI
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4024523Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4024831Z     ## O que é o plugin-sdk
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4025113Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4026475Z     O [DK22Pac/plugin-sdk](https://github.com/DK22Pac/plugin-sdk) é um SDK C++ que expõe as classes internas do GTA SA como headers C++ compiláveis.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4028629Z     Permite criar ficheiros `.asi` (DLLs injectadas pelo ASI Loader) que correm como código nativo dentro do processo do jogo, com acesso total às estruturas internas.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4030159Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4030375Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4030563Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4031013Z     ## Comparação directa: CLEO vs plugin-sdk
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4031318Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4031562Z     | Capacidade | CLEO (actual) | plugin-sdk (ASI) |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4031899Z     |---|---|---|
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4032369Z     | **Frequência de actualização** | ~300 ms (wait mínimo do loop) | Per-frame (~16 ms a 60 fps) |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4033484Z     | **Speed adaptativa para curvas** | ❌ Sem opcode para ler o multiplicador do link | ✅ `CCarCtrl::FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(m_nStraightLineDistance)` |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4034659Z     | **Detecção automática de offroad** | ⚠️ Só com guard 00EC + 04D3 (manual, 300 ms) | ✅ `CCarCtrl::FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo` per-frame |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4035674Z     | **Alinhamento de faixa** | ⚠️ Só activo em DriveMode=Accurate (CIVICO-C) | ✅ `CCarCtrl::ClipTargetOrientationToLink` em qualquer modo |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4037107Z     | **Controlo de velocidade** | ⚠️ `00AD` (float arredondado, aplicado pelo CLEO) | ✅ `CAutoPilot::m_nCruiseSpeed` (char, escrita directa, precisa) |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4037997Z     | **Múltiplos recrutas** | ❌ 1 recruta (1 handle, 1 estado) | ✅ Até 7 via `PoolIterator<CPed>` |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4038655Z     | **Leitura de CarMission** | ❌ Sem opcode | ✅ `CAutoPilot::m_nCarMission` |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4039274Z     | **Detecção de highway** | ❌ Sem opcode | ✅ `CPathNode::m_bHighway` / `m_bNotHighway` |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4039879Z     | **Nível de tráfego do nó** | ❌ Sem opcode | ✅ `CPathNode::m_nTrafficLevel` |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4040605Z     | **Pathfinding completo** | ⚠️ 04D3 só o nó mais próximo | ✅ `CPathFind::DoPathSearch` (rota completa nó-a-nó) |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4041252Z     | **Coexistência com CLEO** | — | ✅ Corre em paralelo, sem conflito |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4041616Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4041802Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4041970Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4042248Z     ## Estruturas do plugin-sdk utilizadas no protótipo
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4042537Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4042806Z     ### `CAutoPilot` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CAutoPilot.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4043087Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4043378Z     Offset  Campo                    Tipo              Descrição
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4043884Z     0x14    m_nCurrentPathNodeInfo   CCarPathLinkAddress  Link actual (para ClipToLink e SpeedMult)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4044410Z     0x27    m_nCurrentLane           char              Faixa actual
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4044977Z     0x29    m_nCarDrivingStyle       eCarDrivingStyle  0=StopForCars, 2=AvoidCars, 3=PloughThrough, 4=SFI
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4045722Z     0x2A    m_nCarMission            eCarMission       Missão actual (8=GotoCoords, 12=Accurate, 11=StopForever...)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4046616Z     0x40    m_nCruiseSpeed           char              Velocidade de cruzeiro (escrita directa, ~km/h)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4047273Z     0x4D    m_nStraightLineDistance  char              Distância em linha recta ao nó seguinte
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4047786Z     0x5C    m_vecDestinationCoors    CVector           Destino actual da IA
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4048271Z     0x8C    m_pTargetCar             CVehicle*         Carro-alvo (para EscortRear/FollowFar)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4048649Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4048810Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4049086Z     ### `eCarMission` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/eCarMission.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4049444Z     Missões relevantes para o nosso mod:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4049716Z     - `MISSION_NONE = 0`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4050048Z     - `MISSION_GOTOCOORDS = 8` — equivalente ao 00A7 do CLEO
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4050498Z     - `MISSION_GOTOCOORDS_ACCURATE = 12` — equivalente ao 02C2 do CLEO
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4050928Z     - `MISSION_STOP_FOREVER = 11` — parar completamente
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4051322Z     - `MISSION_34 = 52` — FollowCarFaraway (06E1 CIVICO-E)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4051902Z     - `MISSION_43 = 67` — EscortRearFaraway (06E1 CIVICO-D)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4052193Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4052478Z     ### `eCarDrivingStyle` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CAutoPilot.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4052783Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4053277Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS              = 0   // pára para carros, respeita semáforos
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4053706Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_SLOW_DOWN_FOR_CARS         = 1
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4054198Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_AVOID_CARS                 = 2   // desvia, ignora semáforos (CIVICO-D/E/DIRETO)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4054700Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_PLOUGH_THROUGH             = 3   // ignora tudo (offroad)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4055329Z     DRIVINGSTYLE_STOP_FOR_CARS_IGNORE_LIGHTS= 4   // pára obstáculos, ignora semáforos (CIVICO-0/A/B/C/F)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4055776Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4055939Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4056465Z     ### `CCarCtrl` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CCarCtrl.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4056809Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4057136Z     **`FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo(CVehicle*, CNodeAddress&, CNodeAddress&)`**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4057663Z     Encontra os 2 nós de estrada mais próximos ao carro.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4058176Z     Se o nó mais próximo está a >30 m → carro está fora de estrada (canal, montanha).  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4058611Z     CLEO não tem equivalente directo.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4058854Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4059305Z     **`ClipTargetOrientationToLink(CVehicle*, CCarPathLinkAddress, char lane, float* heading, float fwdX, float fwdY)`**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4060011Z     Ajusta `heading` para ser paralelo à faixa de estrada do link actual.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4060589Z     Internamente activo só em `DriveMode=Accurate`. Via ASI aplicamos a qualquer modo.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4061148Z     Resultado: recruta não corta curvas, mantém-se na faixa correcta.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4061483Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4061778Z     **`FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(char straightLineDist)`**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4062279Z     Lê o limite de velocidade do link para a curva actual.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4062767Z     Retorna `float [0.0, 1.0]`: 1.0 = reta, <1.0 = curva (proporcional ao ângulo).  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4063276Z     Permite pre-abrandar antes da curva — impossível em CLEO.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4063798Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4064142Z     **`StopCarIfNodesAreInvalid(CVehicle*)`**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4065006Z     Para o carro imediatamente se os nós do seu AutoPilot estão inválidos.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4066374Z     Útil como guard de segurança quando offroad.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4066923Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4067872Z     ### `CPathNode` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CPathNode.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4068432Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4069092Z     m_bHighway          — nó de auto-estrada (permitir velocidades mais altas)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4069918Z     m_bNotHighway       — explicitamente não-highway
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4070644Z     m_nTrafficLevel     — densidade de tráfego [0-3]
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4071476Z     m_bWaterNode        — nó aquático (recruta nunca deve navegar aqui)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4072743Z     m_bDontWander       — nó que NPCs civis evitam
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4073302Z     m_nNumLinks         — número de ligações do nó
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4073607Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4074175Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4075016Z     ### `CPools` / `PoolIterator` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CPools.h` + `shared/extensions/PoolIterator.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4075743Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4076360Z     // Iterar todos os peds activos no jogo (range-based for):
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4077019Z     for (CPed* ped : *CPools::ms_pPedPool) {
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4077724Z         // ped é garantidamente válido dentro do loop
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4078200Z     }
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4078477Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4079111Z     CLEO não tem equivalente: a varredura manual com `0A8D` é propensa a crashes.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4079726Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4080176Z     ### `CWorld::Players` — `plugin_sa/game_sa/CWorld.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4080938Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4081411Z     CPlayerPed* player = CWorld::Players[0].m_pPed;  // jogador 0 (CJ)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4081975Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4082254Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4082966Z     ### `Events::gameProcessEvent` — `shared/Events.h`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4083446Z     ```cpp
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4083663Z     Events::gameProcessEvent += []() {
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4083974Z         // Corre a cada frame do game loop de SA (~60 fps)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4084325Z         // Equivalente a estar dentro de CGame::Process()
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4084614Z     };
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4084777Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4084937Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4085099Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4085258Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4085500Z     ## O que o protótipo implementa
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4085743Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4085952Z     O ficheiro `grove_recruit_asi.cpp` demonstra:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4086488Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4086803Z     1. **Detecção automática de offroad** (`DetectOffroad`)  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4087298Z        - `FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo` → nó mais próximo → distância  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4087721Z        - Se >30 m: muda para `PloughThrough` + velocidade 60
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4088015Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4088257Z     2. **Speed adaptativa para curvas** (`AdaptiveSpeed`)  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4088693Z        - `FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes(m_nStraightLineDistance)`  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4089139Z        - Multiplica a velocidade base pelo factor da curva
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4089422Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4089652Z     3. **Zona STOP/SLOW per-frame** (`ProcessRecrutaFrame`)  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4090008Z        - Escreve `m_nCruiseSpeed = 0` quando dist < 6 m  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4090344Z        - Escreve `m_nCruiseSpeed = 12` quando dist < 10 m  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4090679Z        - CLEO faz isto a 300 ms; ASI faz per-frame (16 ms)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4090970Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4091212Z     4. **Alinhamento de faixa** (`GetLaneAlignedHeading`)  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4091667Z        - `ClipTargetOrientationToLink` → heading alinhado à faixa  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4092169Z        - Por implementar (v2): aplicar ao steering do veículo directamente
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4092519Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4092776Z     5. **Multi-recruta via PoolIterator** (`ScanPlayerGroup`)  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4093117Z        - Itera `CPools::ms_pPedPool`  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4093461Z        - Filtra `m_nPedType == 7` (GANG1) + `bInVehicle` + proximidade  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4093859Z        - Gere até 7 recrutas em simultâneo
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4094107Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4094265Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4094435Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4094671Z     ## O que falta para versão completa (v2)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4094924Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4095171Z     1. **Comunicação bidirecional CLEO ↔ ASI**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4095947Z        Usar `0A8C` (write_memory) no CLEO para escrever flags numa zona de memória partilhada que o ASI lê. Ex: flag do modo activo (29@) → ASI adapta a sua lógica.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4096896Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4097183Z     2. **Aplicar heading de ClipTargetOrientationToLink ao steering**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4097703Z        Requer hook em `CAutomobile::ProcessControl` ou escrita directa em `m_fSteerAngle`.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4098101Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4098503Z     3. **Verificação de membership via `CPedGroupMembership::IsMember`**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4099058Z        Em vez de apenas verificar `m_nPedType == 7`, verificar que o ped pertence ao grupo nativo do jogador.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4099504Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4099766Z     4. **Detecção de highway → velocidade mais alta**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4100231Z        Ler `CPathNode::m_bHighway` do nó actual → se highway, `SPEED_MAX = 80`.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4100585Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4100831Z     5. **Pathfinding completo com `CPathFind::DoPathSearch`**  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4101344Z        Calcular rota óptima nó-a-nó em vez de depender do 04D3 (nó mais próximo).
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4101883Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4102050Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4102211Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4102378Z     ## Como compilar
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4102573Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4102729Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4103011Z     Requisitos:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4103229Z       - Windows com Visual Studio 2019/2022
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4103570Z       - git clone https://github.com/DK22Pac/plugin-sdk.git
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4103879Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4104087Z     Visual Studio > Propriedades do Projecto:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4104386Z       C/C++ > Additional Include Directories:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4104659Z         $(plugin_sdk)\plugin_sa

2026-03-15T02:36:30.4110555Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4110871Z     ## HÍBRIDO (CLEO + ASI) vs STANDALONE (ASI puro) — qual é melhor?
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4111191Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4111358Z     ### Resposta curta
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4111608Z     **Agora: híbrido (CLEO + ASI).  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4111883Z     A longo prazo: standalone ASI puro.**
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4112133Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4112290Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4112447Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4112771Z     ### Opção A — Híbrido: ASI ao lado do CLEO ✅ (recomendado agora)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4113074Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4113422Z     O ASI corre **em paralelo** com o `grove_recruit_follow.cs` sem substituí-lo.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4113786Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4113964Z     | Responsabilidade | Quem gere |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4114201Z     |---|---|
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4114446Z     | Teclas Y/U/G/H/N/B (recrutar, armar, etc.) | **CLEO** |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4114792Z     | Spawn do recruta, entrada no carro | **CLEO** |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4115153Z     | Modo de seguimento (tecla 4): CIVICO/DIRETO/PARADO | **CLEO** |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4115602Z     | Velocidade per-frame, offroad automático | **ASI** |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4115930Z     | Speed adaptativa para curvas | **ASI** |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4116395Z     | Alinhamento de faixa | **ASI** |
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4116641Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4116861Z     **Porquê é o melhor agora:**
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4117361Z     - O `grove_recruit_follow.cs` já funciona com a máquina de estados completa (8 modos, guards, etc.)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4118068Z     - O ASI apenas **refina** o que o CLEO não consegue fazer (per-frame, leitura de estruturas internas)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4118576Z     - Zero risco de quebrar o que já funciona
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4119000Z     - Não é preciso reescrever nada — instalas o `.asi` e fica logo melhor
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4119336Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4119549Z     **Conflito?** Não, porque:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4119875Z     - O ASI escreve `m_nCruiseSpeed` e `m_nCarDrivingStyle` a cada frame
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4120491Z     - O CLEO reescreve esses mesmos campos **apenas quando o modo muda** (tecla 4)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4121077Z     - Nos frames entre mudanças de modo (que são a maioria), o ASI afina continuamente
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4121456Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4121724Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4121890Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4122250Z     ### Opção B — Standalone: ASI substitui o CLEO inteiramente 🔮 (versão futura)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4122615Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4122923Z     Nesta opção o `.asi` faz **tudo**: teclas, spawn, IA, modos, guards.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4123247Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4123459Z     **Vantagens sobre o híbrido:**
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4123934Z     - Sem limitações do CLEO: teclas per-frame (sem wait 300ms), múltiplos recrutas nativamente
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4124566Z     - Sem necessidade de ter CLEO instalado (menos dependências para o utilizador final)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4125097Z     - Acesso a `CKeyboard`/`CControllerConfigManager` para input mais responsivo
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4125675Z     - `CPedGroupMembership::IsMember` para membership correcto (sem hack de `m_nPedType == 7`)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4126494Z     - Pathfinding completo com `CPathFind::DoPathSearch` para rota real nó-a-nó
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4126882Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4127139Z     **O que faltaria implementar (não trivial):**
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4127551Z     1. Sistema de teclas: `GetAsyncKeyState` ou hook em `CControllerConfigManager`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4128017Z     2. Spawn do recruta: `CCarGenerator` / `CPopulation::AddPed`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4128492Z     3. Máquina de estados (8 modos, guards STOP/SLOW, 00EC offroad 50m)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4128857Z     4. Dialog/texto de "Press Y to enter vehicle"
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4129146Z     5. Sistema de armas: `GiveWeapon`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4129406Z     6. Cleanup na morte/sair do carro
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4129638Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4130004Z     **Conclusão:** Possível e seria a solução mais limpa, mas é semanas de trabalho.  
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4130579Z     O híbrido permite chegar lá **iterativamente** sem reescrever tudo de uma vez.
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4130943Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4131102Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4131262Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4131489Z     ### Recomendação prática
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4131707Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4131862Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4132081Z     Fase 1 (agora): CLEO + ASI híbrido
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4132460Z       grove_recruit_follow.cs  →  gestão de estados, input, spawn
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4132926Z       grove_recruit_asi.asi    →  velocidade per-frame, offroad, curvas
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4133251Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4133482Z     Fase 2 (opcional): migrar estado por estado para o ASI
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4133903Z       Começar pelo input (teclas) → depois spawn → depois modos
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4134324Z       Cada migração desactiva a secção equivalente no CLEO
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4134614Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4134901Z     Fase 3 (versão final): standalone .asi puro, CLEO removido
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4135212Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4135371Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4135528Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4135684Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4135937Z     ## Coexistência CLEO + ASI (modo híbrido actual)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4136431Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4136766Z     O ASI **não substitui** o script CLEO — funciona **ao lado** dele:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4137092Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4137404Z     - **CLEO** gere: teclas Y/U/G/H/N/B, spawn, entrada no carro, estados, modo actual (29@)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4137909Z     - **ASI** gere: velocidade per-frame, offroad auto, alinhamento de faixa
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4138254Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4138460Z     Não há conflito porque:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4138779Z     - O ASI escreve apenas `m_nCruiseSpeed` e `m_nCarDrivingStyle`
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4139236Z     - O CLEO reescreve estes campos quando o modo muda (tecla 4), sobrepondo-se
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4139708Z     - Entre mudanças de modo, o ASI afina continuamente
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4140159Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4140321Z     ---
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4140482Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4140673Z     ## Diagrama de fluxo do ASI
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4140896Z     
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4141056Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4141445Z     [Events::gameProcessEvent] → a cada frame (~60fps)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4141748Z       │
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4141980Z       ├─ [60 frames] → ScanPlayerGroup()
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4142375Z       │    └─ PoolIterator → encontrar peds GANG1 em veículo próximo
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4142783Z       │         └─ preencher g_recrutas[0..6]
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4143048Z       │
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4143315Z       └─ [cada frame] → para cada recruta em g_recrutas:
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4143711Z            ├─ dist < 6m  → CruiseSpeed=0, MISSION_STOP_FOREVER
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4144078Z            ├─ dist < 10m → CruiseSpeed=12 (SLOW)
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4144402Z            ├─ [30 frames] → DetectOffroad()
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4144761Z            │    └─ FindNodesThisCarIsNearestTo → dist > 30m?
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4145191Z            │         ├─ offroad=true  → CruiseSpeed=60, PloughThrough
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4145603Z            │         └─ offroad=false → AdaptiveSpeed() + AvoidCars
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4146030Z            │                └─ FindSpeedMultiplierWithSpeedFromNodes
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4146701Z            └─ se missão Accurate → GetLaneAlignedHeading()
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4147118Z                 └─ ClipTargetOrientationToLink → heading da faixa
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4147419Z     ```
2026-03-15T02:36:30.4147599Z     <exited with exit code 0>

2026-03-15T02:37:17.0931362Z copilot: Now I have everything I need:
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0931611Z 
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0931836Z 1. **CPedAcquaintance** in the plugin-sdk has `m_nRespect` as `unsigned int`
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0932339Z 2. **CPed** has `CPedAcquaintance m_acquaintance;` as a direct member  
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0932801Z 3. **PED_TYPE_PLAYER1 = 0** in the enum, so `(1 << 0) = 1u` is the flag to set
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0933517Z 4. The `Respects()` check does: `m_pPed->GetAcquaintance().GetRespect() & (1 << playerPed->m_nPedType)` = `m_acquaintance.m_nRespect & 1u` for player ped
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0934338Z 
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0934342Z 
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0934952Z 5. The fix is to set the respect flag directly with `g_recruit->m_acquaintance.m_nRespect |= 1u;`, and CPedAcquaintance is already accessible through CPed.h
2026-03-15T02:37:17.0935497Z
